channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: India's richest person wants to build the world's largest data center, five times the capacity of Microsoft's biggest site
item.description: Mukesh Ambani wants to build the world's largest data center, five times the capacity of Microsoft's mega site.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Everything new on Disney Plus in February 2025: Pixar's Win or Lose, A Thousand Blows, and more
item.description: From Pixar's first-ever TV show to more episodes of Marvel's new Spider-Man series, here's what to expect on Disney Plus this month.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: How Trump turned the TikTok ban into a political asset
item.description: President Trump has managed to turn a controversial TikTok ban into a political advantage, framing himself as the savior of the popular video-sharing platform by offering the app a 75-day reprieve.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Squid Game season 3: what we know so far about the hit Netflix show's return
item.description: Squid Game season 3 will officially launch on Netflix in June – here's all you need to know before it's released.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Texas governor orders ban on DeepSeek, RedNote for government devices
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas Republican Gov. Greg Abbott issued a ban on Chinese artificial intelligence company DeepSeek for government-issued devices, becoming the first state to restrict the popular chatbot in such a manner.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UPS to Cut Amazon Shipments by 50%, Citing Unprofitability - TechRadar Pro
item.description: UPS, the world's largest package delivery company, has announced plans to reduce its shipping volumes for Amazon by more than 50% under a revised agreement with the ecommerce giant.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: HP redefines PC refresh approach using performance data
item.description: Traditionally, a PC refresh plan is scheduled based on the age of the devices, but as technology advances, so do opportunities. PCs today often continue to perform as needed for much longer than established in a lifecycle policy.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Bringing zero downtime to anywhere work
item.description: In a work-from-anywhere world, how can a team of so few be expected to keep doing so much for so many? The pace is unsustainable. It’s time for IT to enlist some support of its own and delegate—handing off time-consuming tasks to people and to intelligent, automated tools.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How Apple Vision Pro is finding a home in healthcare
item.description: In early 2024, Ryan Broderick, a surgeon at UC San Diego Health, was commiserating with some colleagues about the profusion of screens in today’s operating rooms. Though the displays provided essential guidance for minimally invasive operations in progress, they also added complexity.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: RFK Jr. kept asking to see the science that vaccines were safe. After he saw it, he dismissed it
item.description: The man who hopes to be President Donald Trump’s health secretary says he needs to see data showing vaccines are safe, but he dismissed the evidence shown by an influential Republican senator.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard And Mike Stobbe, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Apple's iPhone sales during the holiday season slipped despite a highly anticipated AI rollout
item.description: Apple on Thursday disclosed its iPhone sales dipped slightly during the holiday-season quarter, signaling a sluggish start to the trendsetting company’s effort to catch up to the rest of Big Tech in the race to bring artificial intelligence to the masses.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US cybersecurity agency's future role in elections remains murky under the Trump administration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nation’s cybersecurity agency has played a critical role in helping states shore up the defenses of their voting systems, but its election mission appears uncertain amid sustained criticism from Republicans and key figures in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Coming soon
item.description: The Deepseek-R1 model has managed to attract a lot of attention in a short time, especially because it can be used commercially without restrictions.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Italy blocks access to the Chinese AI application DeepSeek to protect users' data
item.description: Italy’s data protection authority has blocked use of Chinese tech startup DeepSeek’s AI application to protect Italians’ data and announced an investigation into the companies behind the chatbot.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Why artificial intelligence could cause the lights to dim
item.description: "Data centers is one of the new industrial sectors that we've been looking at because it's part of the emerging economy and new technologies," said Arman Shehab The post Why artificial intelligence could cause the lights to dim first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Amazon sues state agency to block release of company records to Bezos-owned Washington Post
item.description: Amazon is suing a Washington state agency to prevent the release of some company materials to The Washington Post, the newspaper which is owned by Amazon founder Jeff Bezos.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Reid Hoffman says that AI can give you superpowers
item.description: Reid Hoffman returns to Rapid Response to explore today’s AI landscape, and the future promised by a concept he calls “superagency.” Hoffman shares his vision for what an AI-infused workday will soon look like, how we should address society’s greatest fears about technology, and more.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: AI investments face integration, compliance, and skills challenges: survey
item.description: Network engineers report that they see the current and future value in using artificial intelligence for network management and cybersecurity purposes, according to new survey results, but they also anticipate more barriers to successful adoption than their C-suite counterparts.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft reports 10% quarterly profit growth as it works to show AI investments paying off
item.description: Microsoft said Wednesday that its profit for the October-December quarter grew 10% as it works to capitalize on the huge amounts of money it has spent to advance its artificial intelligence technology.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 200 UK companies adopt a permanent four-day work week
item.description: Two years after participating in a groundbreaking four-day work week experiment, 200 companies in the UK have chosen to adopt the policy permanently, marking a significant shift in workplace norms.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: General purpose AI could lead to array of new risks, experts say in report ahead of AI summit
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Advanced artificial intelligence systems have the potential to create extreme new risks, such as fueling widespread job losses, enabling terrorism or running amok, experts said in a first-of-its-kind international report Wednesday cataloging the range of dangers posed by the technology
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Industry coalition presses DOGE to act on digital identity
item.description: Digital identity advocates are calling on Trump administration to boost the use of mobile drivers licenses and other digital identity verification use cases. The post Industry coalition presses DOGE to act on digital identity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: IBM targets mainframe customers with prebuilt AI training modules
item.description: IBM is rolling out a portfolio of enterprise data sets aimed at jumpstarting AI development for its Z and LinuxONE mainframe customers.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How to advance federal workforce efficiencies amid shifting priorities
item.description: Agencies can save time and money — and increase hiring success — by moving to modern human resources solutions, workforce tech experts explain. The post How to advance federal workforce efficiencies amid shifting priorities first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Java-based organizations mostly use Java for AI development – report
item.description: Azul Systems, in its Azul 2025 State of Java Survey & Report, says that Java leads Python and JavaScript for AI development in Java shops, based on a survey of 2,039 Java professionals on six continents.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump says Microsoft is one of the companies eyeing TikTok
item.description: President Donald Trump said Monday evening that Microsoft is among the U.S. companies looking to take control of TikTok to help the popular app avert an effective ban that could kick-in in April.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: After alarming audit findings, here’s how HHS can overcome cloud security gaps
item.description: The report highlights the possibility of unauthorized access and control over critical systems. The post After alarming audit findings, here’s how HHS can overcome cloud security gaps first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Sweden seizes vessel suspected of 'sabotage' after undersea data cable rupture in Baltic Sea
item.description: RIGA, Latvia (AP) — Swedish prosecutors announced Sunday night that they have opened a preliminary investigation into suspected aggravated “sabotage” and ordered the detention of a vessel in the Baltic Sea suspected of damaging an underwater fiber optic cable connecting Latvia and the Swedish island
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Crooks can't steal this motorcycle that hides in plain sight
item.description: A nondescript metal box transforms into a fully functional electric motorcycle that blurs the line between vehicle and urban infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
